last_action: all 12 tasks done — 164 python + 1599 jest tests pass, every new test drift-proofed

FR-001.title: Drift reports how many capabilities it checked and how many it skipped

FR-002.title: No in-sync success claim when zero capabilities were checked

FR-003.title: A clean run with >=1 checked still states in sync and names the count

FR-004.title: Skipped count and per-capability reasons reported whether or not any were checked

FR-005.title: Machine-readable output carries checked and skipped counts as data

FR-006.title: Drift continues to exit successfully in all cases

FR-007.title: Drift stays silent when the living-specs feature is disabled

FR-008.title: Fold-back reports applied requirement changes, not parsed ones

FR-009.title: A dropped fold-back change is visible to the reader

FR-010.title: Fold-back counts unchanged when every parsed change applies cleanly

context: area: speckit-extension/scripts/drift.py — render_human summary + compute_drift result shape
constraint: drift never halts, always exits 0 (documented contract)
constraint: living-specs disabled stays silent (LS-1 inert/opt-in contract)
constraint: feature branch must not bump extension.yml version
intent: Make the drift and fold-back summary lines report what actually executed instead of what was attempted, so a skipped or dropped operation can never read as success.
expectations: No sweeping refactor of every reporting surface in speckit-extension/scripts — other instances are audited and reported, not changed
No change to the exit-code contract: drift still always exits 0
No change to the disabled-feature path, which stays silent
No VS Code extension (src/) changes, so no root README/CHANGELOG edits
approach: Carry the outcome alongside the result and render summaries from it. compute_drift gains a checked count; render_human branches on whether anything was actually checked, reserving the success claim for checked>0 and ending an all-skipped run on a counts line. apply_deltas returns per-verb applied counts so the fold logs what landed, not what it parsed.

decisions.decision: All-skipped runs end on a counts line (0 checked, N skipped) with no success glyph; the success claim is narrowed to checked>0 and names the count
decisions.why: The reported harm is a reader who scrolls to the last line, so the honest signal must occupy exactly the position the dishonest one held
decisions.rejected: Keeping the success glyph with a (0 checked) footnote — the defect wearing a footnote

decisions.decision: Exit code stays 0 in every case, including all-skipped
decisions.why: The never-halts contract is documented in the module docstring and README; a skip is correct behavior on adoption day, and one non-zero code could not distinguish skipped-everything from found-drift, which demand opposite responses
decisions.rejected: Exit 2 on all-skipped, and a --strict flag — the new checked field is the primitive a strict flag would be built on, so deferring it stays cheap

decisions.decision: Add an explicit checked integer to the drift result rather than letting consumers infer it from len(capabilities)
decisions.why: --json is documented as the tooling/CI surface, and inferring ran-ness from an array length is the same reasoning error the human-readable bug encodes
decisions.rejected: A boolean ran flag — strictly less informative than a count

decisions.decision: Do not fix the ten sibling reporting surfaces the audit found, including a second mechanically-distinct drift.py instance where a git failure yields a false in-sync verdict
decisions.why: Folding them in would widen the change past its own spec, the exact failure the previous ticket hit; they are reported for separate triage
decisions.rejected: Fixing the second drift.py instance here since it is the same file — it has its own correct-behavior question (skip or hard error) that would leave the spec narrower than the diff

plan.summary: Two summary lines rendered from intent get rerouted through outcome: a checked count on the drift result, and applied-not-parsed counts from delta application
plan.key_finding: The all-skipped bug is structural, not cosmetic — has_drift is vacuously false over an empty capability list, so the success branch is reached by an absence of findings rather than a presence of checks

tasks.summary: 12 tasks across 8 phases: a 2-task foundational wave reshaping the drift result and renderer, one phase per user story, a contained fold-back phase, and a docs phase; every test task carries a drift-proof obligation

implement.summary: Drift summaries and the living-spec fold now report what executed: a checked count gates the success claim, and delta application returns applied-not-parsed counts

T001.did: Added a checked count to the drift result on both the disabled and normal return paths

T002.did: Rewrote the drift summary to branch on the checked count: no success claim at zero checked, a counted claim otherwise, and a counts line on the skipped and drifted paths

T003.did: Test that an all-skipped run states 0 checked and makes no success claim; drift-proofed - fails on assertNotIn('All capabilities in sync') when reverted

T004.did: Tests that a disabled run stays silent and an enabled-but-unconfigured run reports no capabilities rather than success

T005.did: Test that a mixed run reports both the counted success claim and the skipped count

T006.did: Test that a mixed run with drift still reports findings and states the skipped count

T007.did: Re-derived the two existing success-string tests from the new contract rather than loosening them

T008.did: Test that checked is present across disabled/all-skipped/mixed and always equals the capabilities length

T009.did: Test that the command exits zero when every capability was skipped, in both human and json modes

T010.did: Delta application now returns per-verb applied counts and the fold logs those instead of the parsed ones, naming how many changes were dropped

T011.did: Tests for all-dropped, partial, and all-applied folds plus the fold log line; updated the four existing apply_deltas callers for the new return shape

T012.did: Documented the outcome-reporting summary in the spec-kit extension README and added the [Unreleased] changelog entry

verified.result: all 6 suites OK; test_living_specs 164 tests pass (was 158, +11 new, -5 net after restructuring)

verified.what: Drift-proof: reverted drift.py and confirmed the new tests go red
verified.result: 8 drift tests fail; the key one fails on assertNotIn("All capabilities in sync") - the exact lie, not a missing-field KeyError

verified.what: Drift-proof: reverted write-context.py and confirmed the fold tests go red
verified.result: 9 fail; test_fold_log_counts_applied_changes_not_parsed_ones fails on the log line asserting ~0 modified
warnings: test_fold_log_is_unchanged_when_everything_applies passes both ways by design - it is the FR-010 no-regression guard, not a bug repro

verified.what: Original bug reproduction in a sandbox repo
verified.command: python3 speckit-extension/scripts/drift.py --root <sandbox with uncommitted capability specs>
verified.result: before: 2 skips then the green all-clear; after: 0 checked, 2 skipped (spec.md not yet committed), exit 0

verified.what: Build and full VS Code extension suite

verified.result: OK - 14 bodies match parts, golden, and carry the timing fence

concerns.note: An audit of the sibling reporting lines found 10 more instances of the same intent-vs-outcome class, including a second one in drift.py where a failed git diff yields an empty changed set so the capability is reported positively in sync rather than skipped. Deliberately left unfixed to hold this change to its spec; reported for separate filing.
